channel.title: M365 Copilot on Embrace The Red
channel.description: Recent content in M365 Copilot on Embrace The Red

item.title: DEF CON: Copirate 365 - Plundering in the Depths of Microsoft Copilot (CVE-2026-24299)
item.description:
This is a writeup of my DEF CON Singapore talk that walks through vulnerabilities and exploits in M365 and Consumer Copilot, that I responsibly disclosed to Microsoft end of last year, demonstrating data exfiltration and memory exploits.

Contents

This turned out to be a longer post covering the 45 minute talk, I added a quick index so you know what’s in here.

  • Preface: A Brief History of AI Data Exfiltration
    • The Lethal Trifecta and M365 Copilot
    • Extracting System Prompt Information and Tools
  • Chapter 1: HTML Preview as Exfiltration Channel
    • Bypass 1: Background Images via CSS
    • Bypass 2: Loading Fonts via @font-face
    • Bypass 3: Auto-Switching to Preview
    • End-to-End Demos: Stealing Emails with Copilot
    • Take-away: AI Widgets and Host Contracts
  • Chapter 2: Delayed Tool Invocation
  • Chapter 3: M365 Copilot Got Memory!
    • Adding Memories via Prompt Injection
    • Deleting Memories via Prompt Injection
  • Chapter 4: SpAIware Persistence + Data Exfiltration
  • Encore: Hacking Consumer Copilot
  • Epilogue: Take-aways

Let’s dive into it.
